title: Flaky Romantic Ukrainian Prunes Pie
description: Savory, flaky pastry filled with caramelized prunes and aromatic herbs, perfect for a romantic Ukrainian-inspired dessert thats also diabetic-friendly.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together flour and salt. Add cubed butter and, using fingertips, rub into flour until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Gradually add cold water, stirring until dough comes together. Shape into a disk, wrap in plastic, and chill for 20 minutes.
On a lightly floured surface, roll out the dough to fit a 23cm (9in) pie tin. Transfer to the tin and trim edges.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add onion and garlic, sauté until softened, about 3 minutes.
Add prunes to the skillet, cooking until they release juices and begin to caramelize, 5-7 minutes. Stir in dill, oregano, and lemon zest.
Season with pepper and a pinch more salt if desired. Spread the prune mixture evenly over the pastry base.
Roll out a second dough disc and place over the filling. Trim and crimp the edges to seal. Cut small slits in the top to allow steam to escape.
Bake for 35-40 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown and the filling is bubbly.
Allow the pie to rest for 10 minutes before slicing. Serve warm or at room temperature.
Optional: Add a dollop of low-sugar yogurt or a sprinkle of fresh herbs as a finishing touch.
